methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $26M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Helios is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $26M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-stage, same-sector private companies (Watershed, Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) alongside stage-progression methodology. The broader comparable universe includes eight private frontier-tech firms in climate and energy, plus one public company proxy.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression, using the $26M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implies a $52M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the four same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $175M, with a range from $110M (Steady Energy) to $1.8B (Watershed). The wide dispersion reflects differentiation in market position and investor appetite within early-stage climate tech.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $44M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $114M. The low end anchors to stage-progression mechanics; the high end reflects peer median multiples. Key uncertainties include significant variation within the peer set—particularly the $1.8B outlier—and limited visibility into Helios' operational metrics, IP defensibility, and capital efficiency relative to named comparables. A valuation in the $80M–$150M range appears most defensible pending deeper due diligence on unit economics and competitive positioning.
